mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, consumer spending growth decelerates sharply, reducing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand strength, which lifts equity valuations and generates positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive return differential compared to baseline periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.04 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a monthly decline in the index of industrial production indicates capacity constraints and slowing output growth, which triggers profit margin compression and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The negative return signal reverses to positive in late-cycle regimes, or the mechanism shows no predictive power when filtered for expansion phases exceeding 60 months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, creating a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the signal hit rate falls below 50% across all tested expansion and contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises by more than 0.3 percent month-over-month, inflation expectations force the Federal Reserve to accelerate tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 55% of months following CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.3 percent, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms a tightening labor market that boosts household income and consumer confidence, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal produces negative or flat forward returns in over 50% of triggered months, or interaction effects vanish when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing downturn regime, a monthly housing starts figure below 600 thousand units signals collapsing construction activity and reduced consumer leverage, which drags down financial sector earnings and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns during housing downturns, or HOUST levels below 600 thousand coincide with positive equity returns in more than 60% of observed cases.
